title: Pungent Pickled Peruvian Vine Leaves Dumplings
description: A festive Peruvian dish featuring vine leaves, pickled in a brine solution, and served as a whole30-friendly snack, with a winey and chewy texture.
instructions: In a large pot, combine the red wine vinegar, water, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and let the brine solution simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and chopped onion to the brine solution and let it simmer for another 5 minutes.
Prepare the vine leaves by rinsing them in cold water and removing the stems.
Pack the vine leaves tightly into a large jar, leaving about 1 inch of space at the top.
Pour the hot brine solution over the vine leaves, making sure they are completely covered.
Seal the jar and let it cool to room temperature.
Refrigerate the jar and let the vine leaves pickle for at least 24 hours.
To serve, remove the desired number of vine leaves from the jar and drizzle with olive oil.
